calendar.matters:
Petition For Removal Of Trustee; To Instruct Trustee And For Suspension Of Trustee'S Powers
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour for and with Marley Kinney.

The Court noted that no objections have been filed. The Court granted the Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order). The Order to Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee; To Instruct Trustee And For Suspension Of Trustee'S Powers
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Continued To Mar-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Margot Williams and David Mansour for and with Marley Kinney (Petitioner).

Ms. Williams stated that the proof of service was filed on Friday in response to the Probate Examiner's notes, that she served Mr. Kinney by mail on January 12, 2018 at an address in Costa Rica, that she did not serve the Successor Trustee and that what was attached to the petition was everything the petitioner received that Counsel for the Trustee at the time provided. Ms. Williams also stated that there are distinct separate Trusts for each of the Children that survive, that the Petitioner has turned twenty five, that the respondent stated that he has no intent to make distribution and that the financial institution indicated an updated P.O. Box. In addition, Ms. Williams requested that the Court suspend the Trustee and appoint the Petitioner as temporary Trustee with an order that she not distribute any money and for the purpose of safe guarding the funds, and stated that the Cousin is having the same problem. The Court suggested the parties try and get a copy of the Trust from the Aunt. Ms. Kinney stated that she has communicated with Mr. Kinney over facebook, that he stated that he will not turn over the money and that it will mature as she matures. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. and the Order to Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction are continued to March 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for service to the Successor Trustee and service to the P.O. Box. The Court will suspend the Trustee and appoint Ms. Kinney with the power to marshal the assets (proposed order provided in open Court).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee; To Instruct Trustee And For Suspension Of Trustee'S Powers
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Continued To Jan-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Margot Williams for and with Marley Kinney (Petitioner).

Ms. Williams stated that she does not think this matter is ripe, requested that the Court at least suspend the Trustee, stated that Ms. Kinney will have her 25th birthday on December 30th, that she understands there are some service issues, and that the Trustee has left the Country and fails to respond. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. is continued to January 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Continued To Jan-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Margot Williams for Petitioner Marley Kinney

The Court inquires regarding service. Ms. Williams states that she has served by mail to his last known address. The Court responds that there has not been a showing of service in compliance with statute. Ms. Williams asked that the Temporary Restraining Order be extended. The Court observed that extending the order preventing distribution of the funds in trust would prevent him from performing his obligation as trustee. Ms. Williams requests that the preliminary injunction be granted, or alternatively to extend the temporary restraining order. The Court denies the petition for preliminary injunction, but grants the request to extend the temporary restraining order. The oTRO is extended to January 5, 2018, subject to receipt and review of the order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: not present. (Not reported)

Ex Parte Petition For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
